subject: [PATCH v3 0/1] writeback: don't block sync for filesystems with no data integrity guarantees

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Jan Kara noted that the patch looks good and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag, specifically mentioning himself as the reviewer.

Jan Kara noted that for filesystems like FUSE, it's crucial to submit IO asynchronously to prevent hangs on system suspend or when dealing with a broken FUSE server.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good to me. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

I'd just slightly expand the added comment like:

---

For filesystems such as FUSE it is important that IO submission is done
completely asynchronously as e.g. on system suspend or with broken FUSE
server, the IO submission might hang.

								Honza

reviews.author: Christian Brauner
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not address the issue of blocking sync for filesystems without data integrity guarantees, and requested further changes to fix this.
reviews.raw_body:
Applied to the vfs.fixes branch of the vfs/vfs.git tree.
Patches in the vfs.fixes branch should appear in linux-next soon.

Please report any outstanding bugs that were missed during review in a
new review to the original patch series allowing us to drop it.

It's encouraged to provide Acked-bys and Reviewed-bys even though the
patch has now been applied. If possible patch trailers will be updated.

Note that commit hashes shown below are subject to change due to rebase,
trailer updates or similar. If in doubt, please check the listed branch.

tree:   https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/vfs/vfs.git
branch: vfs.fixes

[1/1] writeback: don't block sync for filesystems with no data integrity guarantees
      https://git.kernel.org/vfs/vfs/c/76f9377cd2ab

2026-03-20.patch_summary: This patch prevents the sync operation from blocking for file systems that do not guarantee data integrity. It achieves this by skipping the check in sync_inodes_one_sb() and instead checking in sync_inodes_sb(). This ensures that even if a file system does not ensure data integrity, it will still allow the flusher threads to run during sync operations.
